description: I1 Phase 2: External validation of the Functional Proximity Law on the p53 network using real STRING v12.0 data (proteins_multilayer_string.json). The v1.0 analysis used manually curated edges; this uses raw STRING channel scores as a fully independent data source. Same 15 proteins, same dimensional structure, independently sourced edges. A DENIED result here is a scientifically more interesting boundary condition than a confirmation. Pre-registration committed before running any analysis on the STRING graph.
pre_registration_note: Predictions made May 1, 2026. The v1.0 I1 analysis is complete (verdicts recorded). This file pre-registers hypotheses for the independent STRING v12.0 replication. The only permissible calibration is the existence of the 4-layer STRING graph — no r values, no hub rankings from the STRING data have been inspected before these hypotheses were written.

hypotheses.statement: r(functional_association, physical_interaction) > r(functional_association, co_expression) in the STRING v12.0 graph. The functionally-measured layers (FA and PI) will have higher hub persistence between them than between FA and the computationally-derived co_expression layer. This replicates the h4 CONFIRMED result from v1.0 on independent STRING data.
hypotheses.rationale: This is the core law test on independent data. FA (dscore, KEGG/Reactome/GO pathway membership) and PI (escore, experimental binding) both encode direct biochemical reality at the same structural resolution. co_expression (ascore, mRNA correlation) is a computational proxy that operates at a different measurement level. The resolution boundary condition predicts: same-resolution layers persist more strongly.

hypotheses.statement: TP53 will be the top degree hub in the physical_interaction layer of the STRING graph. This is the most constrained replication test: a change in data source (from curated to raw STRING escore) must not change the identity of the network's structural center.
hypotheses.rationale: TP53 is the master tumor suppressor with the highest number of experimentally confirmed binding partners in the p53 network. The STRING escore reflects the same class of experimental evidence (co-IP, crystal structure, pulldown). Hub topology at this scale is data-source-robust if the biological system is correctly represented.

hypotheses.statement: The sign of r(functional_association, physical_interaction) from STRING v12.0 will be positive, matching the sign from v1.0 (v1.0 result: r=+0.749). A sign flip would indicate that the STRING data encodes a structurally incompatible layer definition — a data-level boundary condition, not a law violation.
hypotheses.rationale: The law predicts positive hub persistence between layers encoding functionally similar relationships at the same resolution. If the STRING-sourced edges produce a negative r, the layers are not structurally equivalent to the v1.0 curated layers — this is a falsifiable claim about data equivalence, not about the law itself.

hypotheses.statement: r(functional_association, genetic_interaction_proxy) < r(functional_association, physical_interaction). The genetic_interaction_proxy layer (textmining channel: escore < 0.15 AND tscore >= 0.45) is a literature proxy, not a CRISPR screen. It encodes a different relational regime from direct functional association. Hub persistence from FA to textmining will be lower than from FA to experimental binding.
hypotheses.rationale: The layer description explicitly flags that genetic_interaction_proxy is NOT the same as DepMap CRISPR genetic interaction. Textmining captures literature co-mention patterns — a behavioral signal like co_change in software. By the resolution boundary condition, cross-regime layers (FA vs textmining) should show lower r than same-regime layers (FA vs PI). This is a direct test of the regime distinction.

hypotheses.statement: CHEK1 will appear in the top-3 divergent items between the physical_interaction and genetic_interaction_proxy layers in the STRING graph. In v1.0, CHEK1 was the surprise top diverger (gap=8, physical rank 10 vs genetic rank 3 — falsifying the pre-registered ATM prediction). The prediction here is that CHEK1's layer-divergent behavior is structural — it survives data source change — and is not an artifact of manual curation.
hypotheses.rationale: CHEK1's v1.0 divergence was unexpected but mechanistically explainable: CHK1 is physically peripheral (few direct binding partners in the p53 complex) but genetically central (essential backup checkpoint, highest synthetic lethality effect size). If STRING data encodes the same biological asymmetry, CHEK1 should remain a top diverger. If ATM appears instead, the curation-vs-STRING difference is the explanation.

analysis_plan: 1. Run python dev.py law examples/proteins_multilayer_string.json — get all layer-pair r values
2. Run python dev.py cohesion examples/proteins_multilayer_string.json --null 500 — null-calibration
3. Run python main.py examples/proteins_multilayer_string.json — full engine output including cross_layer_analysis
4. Inspect cross_layer_analysis for: r(FA, PI), r(FA, coexp), r(FA, genetic_proxy) — test h1, h3, h4
5. Inspect degree_centrality for PI layer — test h2 (TP53 rank #1)
6. Inspect layer_divergent_items between physical_interaction and genetic_interaction_proxy — test h5
